qa.user_verbatim_complaint: Can the feedbackcoach be on top then normal company below. Such as: FeedbackCoach / NormalCompany - Marketing Team
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Both inner row wrappers in DashboardHeader (app/page.tsx L10 and L14) switched from inline-flex to flex (block-level). Live measurement at desktop 1440x900: titleRow.bottom=128 <= ncRow.top=136 (8px gap, vertically stacked). Mobile 390x844 same stacking with 8px gap. Both rows centered (offset = 0px from header center). Screenshots confirm 'FeedbackCoach' renders on the top visual line and 'NC NormalCompany — Marketing Team' renders on its own line below — exactly the user's mockup.

complaint_element_pointer.selector: main > header > div:nth-child(1) (title row, contains Sparkles svg + h1 'FeedbackCoach') AND main > header > div:nth-child(2) (NC row, contains aria-label='NormalCompany' div + span 'NormalCompany — Marketing Team')
complaint_element_pointer.how_located: DOM traversal — header element queried, then :scope > div children enumerated. titleRow contains the H1; ncRow contains the [aria-label='NormalCompany'] circle and the company-team span.

element_screenshots.before_path: docs/dev/screenshots/dashboard-desktop-1440x900.png (Cycle 1 baseline — both rows on single horizontal line at desktop)

qa.overall_assessment: Cycle 2 fully satisfies the user's verbatim ask. Two inline-flex -> flex substitutions in app/page.tsx L10/L14 produce vertical stacking on both desktop 1440x900 (gap 8px, both rows centered offset 0px) and mobile 390x844 (gap 8px, both rows centered offset 0px). All Cycle 1 deliverables (CHANGE 1 H1 36px bold 'FeedbackCoach' centered header, CHANGE 2 NC circle 32x32 bg-blue-600 with white 'NC' adjacent to company text, CHANGE 3 Briefcase tenure cards) remain intact — no regression. Zero console errors, zero warnings. TypeScript clean. Build/diff matches dev report exactly (1 file, 2 lines changed).

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Can the feedbackcoach be on top
success_criteria_results.criterion: Title row (Sparkles + 'FeedbackCoach') renders on its own block-level line, horizontally centered, on both desktop 1440x900 and mobile 390x844.
success_criteria_results.verification_method: browser_evaluate getComputedStyle(titleRow).display + getBoundingClientRect at both viewports
success_criteria_results.details: titleRow.display='flex' (block-level) at both 1440x900 and 390x844. Desktop: rect top=88, bottom=128, width=848 (fills header content-width); center offset from header center = 0px. Mobile: top=72, bottom=112, width=343; center offset = 0px. Sparkles svg is children[0] and h1 is children[1] within the title row (adjacency preserved).
success_criteria_results.evidence: Desktop measurement: {display:'flex', rect:{top:88,bottom:128,width:848}, centerOffsetFromHeader:0}. Mobile measurement: {display:'flex', rect:{top:72,bottom:112,width:343}, centerOffsetFromHeader:0}. Screenshots cycle2-dashboard-desktop.png and cycle2-dashboard-mobile.png.

success_criteria_results.criterion: NC + 'NormalCompany — Marketing Team' row renders on its own block-level line BELOW the title row, horizontally centered, on both viewports.
success_criteria_results.verification_method: browser_evaluate getComputedStyle(ncRow).display + bounding-box top/bottom comparison vs titleRow
success_criteria_results.details: ncRow.display='flex' at both viewports. Desktop: ncRow.top=136 strictly greater than titleRow.bottom=128 (8px gap from mt-2). Mobile: ncRow.top=120 > titleRow.bottom=112 (8px gap). NC circle [aria-label='NormalCompany'] is children[0] and company-text span is children[1] (adjacency preserved). Both rows centered (offset 0px from header center).
success_criteria_results.evidence: Desktop: titleRow.bottom=128, ncRow.top=136, vertically_stacked=true, gap=8. Mobile: titleRow.bottom=112, ncRow.top=120, vertically_stacked=true, gap=8. Both ncRow.centerOffsetFromHeader=0.

success_criteria_results.source_phrase: Center the entire header section (Cycle 1 Section 5 — must not regress)
success_criteria_results.criterion: Both rows centered within header (each row's center within ±2px of header center).
success_criteria_results.verification_method: browser_evaluate (box.left + box.width/2) - (header.left + header.width/2) for both rows at both viewports
success_criteria_results.details: Desktop 1440x900: header center X=720; titleRow center X=720 (offset 0px); ncRow center X=720 (offset 0px). Mobile 390x844: titleRow centerOffset=0px; ncRow centerOffset=0px. <header> retains text-center (Cycle 1 preserved) and each flex row uses justify-center (Cycle 1 preserved).
success_criteria_results.evidence: All four offsets measured = 0px, well within ±2px tolerance.

success_criteria_results.criterion: H1 'FeedbackCoach' at 36px bold; NC circle 32x32 bg-blue-600 white; tenure cards with Briefcase icon + 'N year[s] at NormalCompany' for all 4 employees; page title 'FeedbackCoach'; zero console errors.
success_criteria_results.verification_method: browser_evaluate getComputedStyle for h1 and NC circle; DOM snapshot for tenure cards; document.title; browser_console_messages
success_criteria_results.details: H1: text='FeedbackCoach', fontSize=36px, fontWeight=700, color=rgb(15,23,42). NC circle: width=32px, height=32px, backgroundColor=rgb(37,99,235) (bg-blue-600), color=white, borderRadius=9999px, fontWeight=700, text='NC'. Tenure cards: all 4 employees show Briefcase img + correct tenure string ('1 year at NormalCompany' Ken, '7 years' Jenny, '5 years' John, '2 years' Clara). Page title 'FeedbackCoach'. Console: 0 errors, 0 warnings on the fresh :3055 server.
success_criteria_results.evidence: DOM snapshot at /, browser_evaluate computed styles, browser_console_messages level=warning returned 0 messages.

qa._success_criteria_rule: BA's requirements_decomposition has 2 items (R1, R2); BA spec also lists 4 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC4). All 4 verified with measured evidence.

root_cause_verification.rationale: Root cause per BA: 'inline-flex' on both inner row wrappers caused inline-level layout, which collapses two siblings onto a single horizontal line on wide viewports. Cycle 2 changed both wrappers to 'flex' (block-level). Live measurement confirms display:'flex' on both rows and stacking is now achieved by normal block flow at both target viewports. The fix is at the same source location BA identified (app/page.tsx:10 and L14) and addresses the exact mechanism (inline-level -> block-level).

build_verification.build_command: npx tsc --noEmit + Next.js dev build (incremental on PORT=3055)
build_verification.deploy_command: PORT=3055 timeout 120 npm run dev (foreground; auto-terminates)
build_verification.build_output_summary: tsc --noEmit exit 0, zero output. Next.js: 'Ready in 2s' on PORT 3055 (Next.js 14.2.35). Pre-existing :3010 dev server had stale CSS/JS chunks (CSS 404), so QA spawned fresh server to verify against post-Cycle-2 source.

notes: Existing :3010 dev server (PID 458805 from Cycle 1 QA) had stale CSS/JS chunks 404'ing — Tailwind utilities did not load on it. QA started a fresh foreground 'PORT=3055 timeout 120 npm run dev' (PID 722544) to verify against the actual post-Cycle-2 source. Verification was performed against :3055 only.

Safety hook blocked an explicit pkill of the fresh :3055 server. The 'timeout 120' wrapper guarantees self-termination ~12:46 UTC. The cleanup-orphan-next-server.timer (per /root/feedbackforge/CLAUDE.md) reaps any deep orphans hourly.
Existing :3010 and production :3025 servers were NOT touched by QA.
